content.problemStatement:
You are given a range of positive integers L,L+1,…RL, L+1, \ldots RL,L+1,…R. Find an integer XXX greater than 111 which is coprime with all the integers in this range (does not share any common factors greater than 111 with any of these integers). In addition, XXX must not exceed 2⋅1092 \cdot 10^92⋅109.

If there are multiple solutions satisfying these conditions, you may find any one of them. It is guaranteed that for the given constraints, at least one such integer XXX exists.

For example, if L=16L = 16L=16 and R=17R = 17R=17, then X=9X = 9X=9 is coprime with both 161616 and 171717. However, if L=6L = 6L=6 and R=12R = 12R=12 instead, then X=9X = 9X=9 shares a common factor 333 with 666 and 121212 and a common factor 999 with 999.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer XXX which satisfies the above conditions.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: X=9X = 9X=9 does not share any common factors with 161616 and 171717.
Example case 2: X=27X = 27X=27 does not share any common factors with 161616 and 171717.
Example case 3: X=79X = 79X=79 does not share any common factors with any of the integers 973360,973361,…,973432973360, 973361, \ldots, 973432973360,973361,…,973432.
